article_title: REGENXBIO's RGX-202 Gene Therapy Meets Primary Endpoint in Pivotal Duchenne Trial as Company Eyes a 2027 Accelerated Approval
summary: Duplicate of the already-published Regenxbio RGX-202 pivotal-trial article (PR #1272). Same May 14 readout, same event; the published sibling is the stronger, more comprehensive piece. Rejected as redundant coverage.

findings.details: https://allsci.com/news/clinical-trials/regenxbios-hits-phase-iii-endpoints-with-gene-therapy-for-duchenne-muscular-dystrophy/ — snapshot failed (403 Forbidden). The two facts the article attributes to allsci (80% of patients >40% expression; common AEs of vomiting, fatigue, nausea) are independently confirmed in the PR Newswire primary source, so they are not unsourced — but the allsci snapshot itself is unverifiable.

Covers the identical May 14, 2026 RGX-202 pivotal AFFINITY DUCHENNE trial readout as the published article 2026-05/14-regenxbios-rgx-202-hits-primary-endpoint-in-pivotal-duchenne-trial-clearing-path-to-a-2027-accelerated-approval-filing (from PR #1272). Two of this submission's four sources (the Regenxbio PR Newswire announcement and BioPharma Dive) are shared with #1272.

content_preview.title: REGENXBIO's RGX-202 Gene Therapy Meets Primary Endpoint in Pivotal Duchenne Trial as Company Eyes a 2027 Accelerated Approval
content_preview.summary: RGX-202 hit its microdystrophin endpoint in the Phase III AFFINITY DUCHENNE trial, but Regenxbio's CEO says he will wait out the FDA leadership transition before filing.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

REGENXBIO announced on May 14, 2026 that its experimental gene therapy for Duchenne muscular dystrophy succeeded in clinical trials, [according to STAT News](https://www.statnews.com/2026/05/14/regenxbio-duchenne-gene-therapy-trial-results/). The company [reported positive topline results](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/regenxbio-announces-positive-topline-results-from-pivotal-phase-iii-affinity-duchenne-study-of-rgx-202-302771834.html) from the pivotal Phase III portion o...

recommendations: No rewrite — the RGX-202 pivotal Duchenne trial readout is already covered by the published article 2026-05/14-regenxbios-rgx-202-hits-primary-endpoint-in-pivotal-duchenne-trial-clearing-path-to-a-2027-accelerated-approval-filing.

editor_notes.content_quality: The article is competently written and structured (645 words, News), with a well-handled Elevidys-positioning angle. Quality is not the reason for rejection.
editor_notes.source_verification: Three of four source snapshots fetched at HTTP 200; the fourth (allsci.com) returned 403 Forbidden and could not be snapshotted. I verified the three available snapshots: source-0 (Regenxbio PR Newswire announcement) confirms 93%/p<0.0001, 71.1%/41.6%/80% expression figures, the subacute myocarditis and asymptomatic liver injury cases, and the Pakola quote; source-1 (BioPharma Dive) confirms the single-arm design, the 28-of-30 biopsy result, the nine-volunteer functional cohort, the 'easily managed and resolved within weeks' and 'upper limit of normal' quotes, the RGX-202-vs-Elevidys construct differences, and Simpson's 'We expect that the new leadership will have a mandate on rare disease flexibility' quote — all verbatim; source-2 (STAT News) is a STAT+ paywalled article whose snapshot captured only the preview, but the visible preview confirms the 'I think our data checks every single box that you would want for accelerated approval' quote and the 'deaths of two recipients from liver failure' Elevidys context. The two facts attributed to the unreachable allsci source (80% of patients >40% expression; common AEs of vomiting/fatigue/nausea) are both independently present in the PR Newswire primary source. Note: this submission shares the Regenxbio PR Newswire announcement and BioPharma Dive sources with the published sibling #1272.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: The facts in the article are accurate and, where the allsci source was unreachable, independently confirmable from the primary source. No fabrication detected. The problem is redundancy, not accuracy.
editor_notes.originality: FAILS. This submission covers the identical event — Regenxbio's May 14, 2026 positive topline readout from the pivotal Phase III portion of the AFFINITY DUCHENNE trial of RGX-202 — as the article published from PR #1272. Same trial, same date, same core results (93%/p<0.0001, the expression figures, the two SAEs, the FDA-leadership-transition framing, the 2027 accelerated-approval target). Two of the four sources overlap. #1272 was selected as the stronger piece because it additionally cites Regenxbio's Q1 2026 financials release — adding the cash position, the 60-patient dosing timeline, and a second Simpson quote — and carries the full NSAA correlation statistics and the named investigator/advocacy quotes.
editor_notes.quote_audit: Rule 3 check passed for the quotes that could be verified. Every quoted passage checked against the available snapshots reproduces source text verbatim with correct attribution. The Pakola quote is a clean truncation ('RGX-202 is the first gene therapy in development for Duchenne to demonstrate') with the paraphrased continuation correctly placed outside the quote marks — no Rule 3 violation. The STAT and BioPharma Dive quotes are exact. Rejection is purely on originality grounds, not quote integrity.
concerns: Duplicate of an already-published article covering the identical RGX-202 pivotal trial readout, with two of four sources overlapping.

One source (allsci.com) was unreachable (403); the facts it was cited for are independently confirmed in the primary source, so this is a minor secondary-source-verifiability issue, not an unsourced-claim issue.
recommendations: No rewrite. The RGX-202 pivotal Duchenne trial readout is already covered by the published, stronger article from PR #1272.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: REJECT as a duplicate. The work is competent and accurate but redundant — the same trial readout is already live in #1272, which is the more comprehensive piece. Under v3.9.0 there is no rewrite cycle for duplicates.
